title: Over-Ear Studio Headphones with Path-Following Spiral Cord
purpose: Reusable product-studio scene for over-ear headphones resting on a polished surface, with opposing cylindrical earcups, a broad flat headband, and a long spiral cord terminating in a stepped jack plug.
preview_note: octane-preview.png is a verified native Octane X render from the one-shot bridge refinement pass.

lighting.intent: dark polished product surface, softbox highlights, contact shadow, restrained metallic reflections

geometry_notes: Single combined OBJ: the Octane build reliably exposes one imported mesh to the active render target.
Earcups use stepped cylindrical shells with planar end closures and opposing axes.
The headband uses a broad flat rectangular profile with the dominant axis rotated 90 degrees.
The cord is a swept spiral around a curved tabletop path, with a decaying radius and a long foreground run.
The jack plug is modeled as a stepped barrel, collars, tapered tip, and strain relief.
quality_checklist: Cups read as opposing cylindrical studio earcups rather than spherical discs.
Headband reads as a broad flat padded band.
Cord visibly follows a path while retaining a continuous spiral.
Cylinder ends are closed and catch specular highlights.
Headphones and cord are grounded by the polished surface and shadow.
Native Octane output is inspected before claiming success.
known_pitfalls: Separate imported meshes are not all connected to the active render target; keep the headphone assembly and polished surface in one OBJ.
OBJ/MTL colours are not sufficient; create and explicitly assign the scene material.
A repeated loop chain is not equivalent to a cord following a path; preserve the swept spiral construction.
The current reliable combined-mesh material route does not independently bind cap metal from the shared scene material.

required_evidence: bridge result metadata for queued commands
native Octane candidate preview at octane-preview.png
final native Octane render bundled as octane-preview.png
stop_conditions: closed ends and grounding are visible
remaining gaps require independent material-group binding

baseline_sweep.purpose: Compare front, three-quarter, cord-emphasis, and top-oblique product framings.

final_bundle.result_metadata_pattern: workspace/results/<command_id>.json copied or summarized into iterations/
